codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers feature requirements. Export endpoint supports JSON format containing all user data (user info, profile, settings, sessions with messages and embedded psych_updates, semantic_assertions). SQLite backup format also supported. 7 comprehensive tests added covering auth, format validation, data structure, and clear functionality. All 369 tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The JSON export includes psych_updates embedded within each message object but does NOT include a top-level memories.psych_updates array (unlike semantic_assertions which have both embedded and top-level representations). This is inconsistent but not a bug since psych_updates data is preserved.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: settings.py:285-292 only queries SemanticAssertions for top-level memories.semantic_assertions. Psych_updates are only in session.messages[].psych_update.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The settings_page hardcodes export format to 'json' despite having a dropdown that could theoretically offer SQLite. The SQLite export endpoint works but cannot be triggered from UI.

summary: Feature implementation complete and correct. POST /api/settings/export returns JSON with all user data (user, profile, settings, sessions with messages and psych_updates, semantic_assertions). DELETE /api/settings/clear-data removes all user data in correct cascade order. 7 tests added covering auth, format validation, data structure verification, and clear functionality. All 369 tests pass. VAL-SETTINGS-003 assertion fulfilled.
